title: City Council Regular Session Meeting 6/16/2026
agendaText: City Council Regular Session Meeting - June 16, 2026 Agenda Full Package Agenda Full Package Open Video in new Window Video City Council Agenda City of Campbell, 70 N. First St., Campbell, California NOTE: This City Council Regular meeting will be conducted in person as well as telecommunication and is compliant with provisions of the Brown Act. The City Council meeting will be live-streamed on Channel 26 (Xfinity) or Channel 88 (Direct TV), the City's website and on YouTube https://www.youtube.com/user/CityofCampbell for those who only wish to view the meeting. Those members of the public wishing to provide public comment virtually are asked to register in advance at: https://www.campbellca.gov/signup . After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting. Members of the public may attend the meeting in person at Campbell City Hall - Council Chambers. Public comment will also be accepted via email at [email protected] until 12:00 p.m. prior to the start of the meeting. Written comments will be posted on the website and distributed to the Council. If you choose to email your comments, please indicate in the subject line “FOR PUBLIC COMMENT” and indicate the item number. REGULAR MEETING OF THE CAMPBELL CITY COUNCIL Tuesday, June 16, 2026 07:00 PM City Hall Council Chamber 1. CALL TO ORDER/ROLL CALL 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE 3. SPECIAL PRESENTATIONS AND PROCLAMATIONS Please Note: Items listed under Special Presentations and Proclamations are considered ceremonial. The City Council will not take action on these matters. 4. COMMUNICATIONS AND PETITIONS 5. ORAL REQUESTS NOTE: This portion of the meeting is reserved for persons wishing to address the City Council on any matter not on the agenda. Persons wishing to address the Council are requested, but not required to complete a Speaker’s Card. Speakers are limited to two (2) minutes. The law generally prohibits the Council from discussion or taking action on such items. However, the Council may instruct staff accordingly regarding Oral Requests. 6. COUNCIL ANNOUNCEMENTS 7. CONSENT CALENDAR NOTE: All matters listed under consent calendar are considered by the City Council to be routine and will be enacted by one motion. There will be no separate discussion of these items unless a request is made by a member of City Council, City staff, or a member of the public. Any person wishing to speak on any item on the consent calendar should ask to have the item removed from the consent calendar prior to the time the Council votes to approve. If removed, the item will be discussed in the order in which it appears. 7.1 Minutes of City Council Executive Session Meeting of June 2, 2026. 1. PostMinutes - Package City Council Executive Session _Jun02_2026.pdf Recommended Action Approve the executive session meeting minutes of June 2, 2026. 7.2 Minutes of City Council Regular Minutes of June 2, 2026. 1. PostMinutes - Package City Council Regular Session Meeting_Jun03_2026.pdf Recommended Action Approve the regular meeting minutes of June 2, 2026. 7.3 Approving Payment of Bills and Claims 1. Report-Approving Bills and Claims 06.16.26.pdf Recommended Action Approve the bills and claims in the amount of $1,723,518.39. 7.4 Resolution Approving the Second Amendment to the Contract with the County of Santa Clara to Provide a Senior Nutrition Program for Fiscal Year 2026-27 and Authorizing the City Manager to Execute the Second Amendment to the Contract (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. FY 2026-27 County Council Report SNP.pdf 2. County Resolution FY 2026-27.pdf 3. Attachment 1 - FY 2026-2027 Budget Justification.pdf 4. DRAFT FY27 A2 DOA Delegated Amendment - City of Campbell - SNP Congregate Meals.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution approving the Second Amendment to the Contract with the County of Santa Clara for the provision of congregate meal services and providing funding for Campbell's Senior Nutrition Program for Fiscal Year 2026-27 and authorizing the City Manager to execute the Second Amendment to the Contract. 7.5 Agreement with Moonchef, LLC to Provide Catered Meals for the Senior Nutrition Program for Fiscal Year 2026-27 (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. FY 2026-27 Moonchef Agreement SNP.pdf 2. Council Resolution MoonChef FY 2026-27.pdf 3. FY 2026-2027 Budget Justification.pdf 4. FY 2026-27 Contract MoonChef (1).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution approving an agreement and authorizing the City Manager to execute the agreement with Moonchef , LLC to provide catered meals for the Senior Nutrition Program during Fiscal Year 2026-2027. 7.6 Authorization to Submit Transportation Fund for Clean Air (TFCA) Program Grant Application for Winchester Boulevard and San Tomas Aquino Road Bike Lanes Project (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report- TFCA grant application for Winchester and STAR bike lanes project.pdf 2. resolution- TFCA grant application Winchester and STAR bike lanes project.pdf Recommended Action Adopt a resolution authorizing the Public Works Director to submit a Transportation Fund for Clean Air (TFCA) Program grant application to Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority (VTA) for the Winchester Boulevard and San Tomas Aquino Road Bike Lanes Project. 7.7 Consider the Adoption of a Resolution Dispensing with the Bidding Procedures Set Forth in Campbell Municipal Code Section 3.20.050, Approving a Consultant Service Agreement with Project Sentinel for Rent Dispute and Mediation Services, and Authorizing the City Manager to execute the Agreement (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report for Consultant Services Agreement with Project Sentinel.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Draft Resolution (Rent Mediation) - Award of Contract.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Draft Project Sentinel Agreement.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution dispensing with the bidding procedures specified in Campbell Municipal Code (CMC) Section 3.20.050, approving a two-year Consultant Services Agreement with Project Sentinel to administer the City’s Rent Dispute and Mediation Program through June 30, 2028 and authorizing the City Manager to execute the agreement. 7.8 Excess Workers' Compensation Coverage Contract Extension (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. 2026.06.19 Excess Workers Compensation Contract.pdf 2. Resolution – Excess Workers’ Compensation.pdf 3. FY 27-28 Excess Workers Compensation Extension Proposal.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution approving a renewal of its current agreement with Midwest Employers' Casualty Company to provide excess worker's compensation liability coverage for Fiscal Years 2027 and 2028 for the estimated annual premium amount of $132,240 and authorizing the City Manager to execute any necessary documents or amendment related to the extension. 7.9 Accept Donation from Dale Scribner for a Bench Installation at the Campbell Community Center Running Track (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - Accept donation from Dale Scribner for a bench installation at the Campbell Community Center Running Track.pdf 2. RESOLUTION bench donation at CCC running track.pdf 3. Attachment 1 - Location of Bench.pdf 4. Budget Adjustment.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a resolution: 1) accepting a $3,000 monetary donation from Mr. Dale Scribner for the installation of a bench at the Campbell Community Center Running Track; and 2) authorizing staff to execute a budget adjustment as necessary. 8. PUBLIC HEARINGS AND INTRODUCTIONS OF ORDINANCES NOTE: Members of the public may be allotted up to two (2) minutes to comment on any public hearing item. Applicants/Appellants and their representatives may be allotted up to a total of five (5) minutes for opening statements and up to a total of three (3) minutes maximum for closing statements. Items requested/recommended for continuance are subject to Council’s consent at the meeting. 8.1 Fiscal Year 2027 Budget and CIP Adoption (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - Fiscal Year 2027 Budget and CIP Adoption.pdf 2. FY 2027 City Manager Transmittlal Letter (ADOPT 06-16-26).pdf 3. Resolution - FY 2027 Operating and Capital Budget (ADOPT 06-16-26).pdf 4. Resolution - FY 2027 CIP Conformance to General Plan (ADOPT 06-16-26).pdf 5. Resolution - FY 2027 Appropriations Limit (ADOPT 06-16-26).pdf 6. CVMF Funding Request.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council, by Resolution, adopt the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 City Operating and Capital budgets, including transfers, in the amounts of $93,229,659 and $13,891,152, for a combined total budget of $107,120,811; and adopt a Resolution adopting findings that the proposed FY 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan (CIP) projects are categorically exempt under the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) and are consistent with the City’s General Plan; approving the five-year Capital Improvement Plan with projects totaling $75,220,892; including new capital projects of $4,202,000 and $7,145,804 as the amount necessary to meet the capital requirements of the City in FY 2027; directing staff to incorporate within the final adopted and published FY 2027 budget document any changes or adjustments pursuant to this public hearing and any other minor corrections prior to the budget’s final publication; and adopt a Resolution establishing the Appropriations Limit for FY 2027 at $104,806,101 pursuant to Article XIIIB of the California State Constitution. 8.2 City Council Compensation 2026 (Ordinance/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - City Council Compensation 2026.pdf 2. Ordinance - City Council Compensation .pdf Recommended Action That the City Council introduce an Ordinance adjusting Councilmember compensation, within the limits set forth by Government Code Section 36516. 8.3 Public Hearing to Consider Adoption of an Interim Ordinance to Extend Interim Ordinance No. 26-2350 Which Established Urgency Measures in Response to SB 79, the Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act of 2025 (File No. PLN-2025-50) (Ordinance / Roll Call Vote) 1. Extending SB 79 Interim Ordinance.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - CC Ord. - SB 79 Extension.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - SB 79 Study Area Changes (8.5x11).pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt an Interim Ordinance, extending Interim Ordinance No. 26-2350, which established urgency measures in response to Senate Bill 79, the Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act of 2025. 8.4 Request for City Council initiation of a General Plan Amendment to change the land use designation of property with an existing single family residence located at 480 Chapman Drive from “Neighborhood Commercial” to “Low Density Residential (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report – GPA Initiation - 480 Chapman Drive .pdf 2. CC Resolution - GPA Authorization - 480 Chapman Dr.pdf 3. 1997 Correspondence.pdf 4. Applicant Letter.pdf Recommended Action Staff recommends that the City Council adopt a resolution authorizing initiation of a General Plan Amendment and allowing a reduction in application fees to an amount of $6,000. 9. NEW BUSINESS 9.1 Calling General Municipal Election for Districts 3, 4 and 5; and requesting Consolidation of Election in Accordance with California Elections Code Section 10418 (Resolution/Roll Call Vote) 1. Report - Calling the Election 2026.pdf 2. Resolution - Calling the Election 2026.pdf Recommended Action That the City Council adopt a Resolution calling the General Municipal Election for November 3, 2026 to elect one Councilmember from District 3, one Councilmember from District 4, and one Councilmember from District 5; and requesting consolidation of election with the Registrar of Voters in accordance with California Elections Code Section 10418; resolving any tie votes by lot; and levying a fee to each candidate for their Candidate Statement. 9.2 Approve and Award a Contract and Authorize the City Manager to Execute a Consultant Services Agreement with GUIDA for City
